chapter_title.ar: باب تَقْدِيمِ النَّوَافِلِ وَ تَأْخِيرِهَا وَ قَضَائِهَا وَ صَلَاةِ الضُّحَى
chapter_title.en: Chapter 85 – Preceding the optional (Salāt) and delaying it, and its payback, and the Salāt at forenoon

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/421)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/421)
text: سَأَلَ أَبُو كَهْمَسٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ يُصَلِّي الرَّجُلُ نَوَافِلَهُ فِي مَوْضِعٍ أَوْ يُفَرِّقُهَا فَقَالَ لَا بَلْ يُفَرِّقُهَا هَاهُنَا وَ هَاهُنَا فَإِنَّهَا تَشْهَدُ لَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ .

en.hubeali: And by this chain, from Muhammad Bin Al-Husayn, from Al-Hakam Bin Miskeen, from Abdullah Bin Ali Al-Sarrad who said,

‘Abu Kahmasy asked Abu Abdullahasws, so he said, ‘Should the man pray optional (Salāts) in one place or different?’ So heasws said: ‘No, but he should pray in different places, over her, and over these, for these (places) would be testifying for him on the Day of Judgment’.
en.sarwar: 18. Through the same chain of narrators as that of the previous Hadith it is narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from al-Hakam ibn Miskin from ’Abd Allah ibn Ali al-Sarrad who has said the following: “Once, abu Kahmas asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘Should a man perform his optional Salah (prayer) in one or in different places?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘No, one should perform them here and there because they testify in one’s favor on the Day of Judgment.’”
